resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def pivotArray(self, nums: List[int], pivot: int) -> List[int]:
        left = [num for num in nums if num < pivot]
        middle = [num for num in nums if num == pivot]
        right = [num for num in nums if num > pivot]
        return left + middle + right
```
solution: ```python
class Solution:
    def pivotArray(self, nums: List[int], pivot: int) -> List[int]:
        left = [num for num in nums if num < pivot]
        middle = [num for num in nums if num == pivot]
        right = [num for num in nums if num > pivot]
        return left + middle + right
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution leverages list comprehensions to efficiently partition the input array into three sub-arrays: elements less than, equal to, and greater than the pivot. Concatenating these sub-arrays results in the rearranged array. This approach has a linear time complexity due to the single pass through the input array and a linear space complexity due to the creation of new lists. The energy implications are low as it involves primarily array operations.
